research_scope: Trade counting semantics investigation — read-only, no code changes

1_what_increments_max_trades_per_day.answer: Both entry ORDER PLACEMENT and position EXIT each increment the counter by 1.

increment_events.timing: Immediately after broker.place_option_order() returns a non-None order object
increment_events.note: Called at ORDER SUBMISSION time, NOT at fill confirmation time. This means a limit order that is later cancelled or rejected still consumed a slot.

increment_events.timing: Inside position_monitor(), when pm.should_exit() returns a reason
increment_events.note: Exit orders bypass check_order entirely — they are never blocked by the risk limit. However they DO increment the counter, reducing available entry slots.

increment_events.timing: Inside eod_liquidate(), when positions are force-closed at session end

does_NOT_increment: Fill confirmation by FillTracker.poll() — the risk parameter is passed to _handle_fill() but never used

docstring_discrepancy.docstring_text: Call when an order is filled to track daily counters.
docstring_discrepancy.actual_behavior: Called at order PLACEMENT for entries (before fill), and at EXIT for closures.
docstring_discrepancy.implication: The docstring states fill-time semantics but entry calls happen before fill confirmation. A placed order that cancels (unfilled) still consumed a trade slot.

1_what_increments_max_trades_per_day.check_order_scope: check_order() is called exactly ONCE in session_runner.py (line 854) — for ENTRY orders only. Exit orders never pass through check_order().

timeline.event: SNOW VWAP entry order placed with broker
timeline.note: scan_and_place() processes SNOW signal first (highest ranked at 10:34)
Counter unchanged at fill confirmation. PM.open() called with actual fill price.

timeline.event: SNOW trailing_stop exit triggered by position_monitor()
timeline.note: Hold=91s. Exit order placed with broker (SELL_TO_CLOSE). Counter increments regardless of exit fill.

timeline.event: Scan cycle: MSFT VWAP signal processed FIRST (score=60, higher rank in scan)
timeline.note: In the same scan cycle, DIA ORB (q=3, sc=53) and DIA VWAP (q=1, sc=53) also present. After MSFT is placed (counter=3), they hit the risk gate.

timeline.event: DIA ORB check_order() in same scan cycle after MSFT placed
timeline.note: Block reason: 'risk: Max trades per day reached: 3/3'. Bridge row #1 of 5 at '3/3'.

timeline.event: DIA VWAP check_order() in same scan cycle
timeline.note: Block reason: '3/3'. Bridge row #2 of 5 at '3/3'.

timeline.event: Next scan cycle: DIA ORB and DIA VWAP checked again
Next scan cycle: DIA VWAP blocked (DIA ORB not evaluated here)
MSFT trailing_stop exit triggered by position_monitor()
timeline.note: Hold=62s. Simultaneously: MSFT loss triggers 15-minute cooldown_after_loss timer for DIA signals.

timeline.event: DIA signals (ORB and VWAP) enter 15-minute cooldown_after_loss
timeline.call: (skipped before reaching risk gate — cooldown check is earlier in pipeline)
timeline.note: 26 ORB + 26 VWAP DIA bridge rows stamped as 'skipped / cooldown_after_loss' during this window. Risk gate is never reached — counter remains 4 but not visible in block reasons.

timeline.event: Cooldown expires. DIA ORB and DIA VWAP return to risk gate.
timeline.note: Block reason transitions to '4/3'. Remains '4/3' for all subsequent 149 blocks.

timeline.event: MSTR ORB signals appear (49 bridge rows)
timeline.note: All 49 MSTR ORB signals blocked as '4/3'. None evaluated on merit.

orb_signals_blocked_before_merit_evaluat.note: Every single ORB signal was blocked by the risk counter. No ORB signal reached quality/liquidity/spread evaluation.

3_effective_session_capacity.formula_explanation: With current semantics (entries + exits each count): each complete round-trip costs 2 counter units. The check fires at entry only (check_order not called for exits). An entry is allowed when counter < max. Exit always succeeds but increments counter. With interleaved entry/exit pattern: max_entries = ceil(max / 2).

interleaved_pattern_analysis.description: Worst case for entry capacity: each entry is followed by an exit before the next entry.

max_3.sequence: E→1, X→2, E→3(check:2<3✓), X→4, E→check:4>=3 BLOCKED

max_5.sequence: E→1, X→2, E→3, X→4, E→5(check:4<5✓), X→6, E→check:6>=5 BLOCKED

concurrent_positions_analysis.description: With max_active_positions=2: two entries can occur before any exit. This is worse.

max_3.note: Same as interleaved — 2 entries max under max=3 regardless of concurrency

max_5.sequence: E1→1, E2→2, X1→3, E3→4(check:3<5✓), X2→5, E4→check:5>=5 BLOCKED

max_10.note: Slightly more entries with concurrency under max=10

A_current.name: Current: count entries (at placement) + count exits
A_current.rule: record_trade() called on: entry order placement + position exit closure
A_current.check_semantics: counter >= max blocks next entry; exits never blocked

B_round_trip.name: Alternative: count completed round-trip positions
B_round_trip.rule: record_trade() would be called only when a position is fully closed (entry+exit pair)
B_round_trip.check_semantics: counter >= max blocks next entry; exits never blocked

C_entry_only.name: Alternative: count entry orders only
C_entry_only.rule: record_trade() would be called only on entry order placement
C_entry_only.check_semantics: counter >= max blocks next entry; exits not counted at all

B_round_trip.note: SNOW completes as a round-trip at 10:37 (counter=1). MSFT completes as round-trip at 11:05 (counter=2). DIA ORB at 11:04 runs during MSFT's open position; counter=1 → can trade. DIA ORB cooldown starts at 11:05 but MSTR ORB arrives at 12:04 with counter=2 < 3.

C_entry_only.note: SNOW entry (counter=1), MSFT entry (counter=2). Third entry slot available. DIA ORB at 11:04 would have been evaluated with counter=2.

A_current_vs_B_round_trip.difference: Under B, ORB slots open after each COMPLETED trade. Risk exposure per trade is identical; total exposure determined by max_active_positions=2.
A_current_vs_B_round_trip.max_concurrent_premium_risk: max_active_positions=2 limits concurrent risk independently of max_trades_per_day

A_current_vs_C_entry_only.difference: Under C, exits free up a slot. 3rd entry (ORB) becomes available after 2 entries. Max exposure slightly higher: 3 entries possible vs 2 under current.
A_current_vs_C_entry_only.safety_note: max_active_positions=2 remains the binding constraint on concurrent positions.

docstring_evidence.text: record_trade(): "Call when an order is filled to track daily counters."
docstring_evidence.interpretation: The word 'filled' strongly implies this should count completed fills, not order submissions. In trading, 'a trade' most naturally means an entry order (one-sided).
docstring_evidence.discrepancy: The actual implementation calls record_trade() at ORDER PLACEMENT for entries, not at fill. This means an order placed but then cancelled (unfilled) consumes a slot.

naming_convention_evidence.industry_convention: 'Trades per day' in systematic trading almost universally means number of round-trip positions opened. A trader who 'made 3 trades today' means opened and closed 3 positions. It does not mean 3 order submissions (which could be 1 entry + 2 re-submissions of the same idea).

class_documentation_evidence.list_position: Listed alongside max_risk_per_trade, max_daily_loss — these are all position-level constraints
class_documentation_evidence.implication: The peer constraints (max_risk_per_trade, max_daily_loss) are evaluated once per ENTRY. Symmetry suggests max_trades_per_day was also intended as an entry-level constraint.

test_evidence.test_max_trades_per_day: test_risk_manager.py lines 113-123: the test calls rm.record_trade() three times then checks that check_order() fails. The test does NOT distinguish entry vs exit — it treats record_trade() as a generic 'something happened' counter. This is ambiguous and consistent with either interpretation.

5_design_intent.most_likely_intent: Based on: (1) the docstring saying 'when an order is FILLED', (2) the naming convention 'trades_per_day' = positions opened, (3) symmetry with other entry-level constraints, the most defensible design intent is: max_trades_per_day = maximum number of ENTRY ORDERS (one-sided) per session, counted at FILL confirmation. The current implementation diverges from this in two ways: (a) entry is counted at placement, not fill; (b) exits also consume slots.
5_design_intent.impact_on_session: On 2026-05-29 with max=3: Under intended (entry-only-at-fill) semantics: 2 entries consumed 2 slots, 1 slot remained. ORB DIA at 11:04 AM would have seen counter=2 < 3 and been evaluated on merit. Under current (placement+exit) semantics: after MSFT placement the counter hit 3, locking out all subsequent signals immediately — before fills even confirmed.
